_comment: Expected Dock evaluation results for the MISCONFIGURED fixture. Captured: 2026-03-18, OpenClaw 2026.3.13, --profile misconfigured. Config: channels.slack.groupPolicy=open, no gateway auth, hooks.internal enabled.

NC-OC-003.note: deny_commands_ineffective not present in this profile — no denyCommands configured

NC-OC-007_new.note: gateway.loopback_no_auth — new checkId not yet in control library

browser_new.note: browser.control_no_auth — new checkId not yet in control library

score_estimate: 100 - 25 (NC-OC-004 critical, open_groups checkIds) - 10 (NC-AUTH-001 high) - 5 (NC-OC-008 medium) = 60, RED

new_checkIds_discovered.note: New critical checkId — fires when groupPolicy=open AND tools.elevated=enabled. Should become a new NC-OC control or be incorporated into NC-OC-004.
New critical checkId — fires when groupPolicy=open AND runtime/fs tools are exposed. Should become a new NC-OC control or be incorporated into NC-OC-004.
New critical checkId — fires when gateway has no auth token. Not in current control library. Add as NC-OC-010 or similar.
New critical checkId — fires when browser control is enabled without gateway auth. Not in current control library. Add as NC-OC-011 or similar.

notes: This fixture revealed 4 new checkIds not in the v0.1 control library. NC-OC-001 remains SKIPPED — hooks.webhooks is an internal OpenClaw webhook receiver, not user-configurable via standard config. NC-OC-001 design needs revisiting.
